nativeRange.habitat: Seasonal wetlands, marshes, and riverbanks in tropical Africa

decorPreferences: open background placement with room to grow tall
bright lighting to develop golden coloration

summary: A demanding gold-to-yellow-green background stem plant from tropical Africa with pink-to-red stems; develops its signature warm 'sunlit' foliage in high-tech planted tanks with strong light, CO2, and iron-rich fertilization.
careNotes: Golden Nesea is the hobby trade name for Nesaea pedicellata 'Golden'. Taxonomy is in flux: modern revisions (Graham & Gandhi 2013) fold the entire genus Nesaea into Ammannia, so this plant is increasingly listed as Ammannia pedicellata 'Golden'. The plant is native to tropical Africa (West African and southern African seasonal wetlands per the Nesaea/Ammannia revision in Lythraceae), and is sold as a midground-to-background stem plant typically reaching 20-40 cm submersed (some retailers cite '12 inches or more', i.e. >30 cm). Sources broadly agree on water parameters of 22-28 C, pH 6.0-7.5 (Enchanted Grow cites 5.5-7.0, slightly more acidic), and soft-to-moderately-hard water (~2-12 dGH; some sources up to 15 dGH). The defining husbandry question is light + CO2: although the species can survive at moderate light without CO2 (Modern Aquarium classifies it 'easy to moderate'), the golden-yellow leaf color and pink-red stems that the plant is sold for only develop reliably under high light (8-10 h photoperiod) with pressurized CO2 (20-30 ppm/mg/L) and consistent iron + micronutrient dosing on top of macros. Under low light it tends toward dull green and lower-leaf drop. Nutrient-rich substrate (aquasoil or root tabs) is strongly recommended for root development, and the plant rewards heavy fertilization with growth of up to 5 cm/week under optimal conditions. Like other Ammannia/Nesaea, it is sensitive to parameter swings and freshly converted tissue-culture stems may melt during emersed-to-submersed transition. Note: Buce Plant's /products/nesaea-golden URL from the discovery manifest returned 404 at research time; the live product page is at /products/nesaea-pedicellata-golden and was not used as primary source. Wikipedia has only a genus-level Nesaea page with no species-level care detail.
breedingNotes: Propagated by stem cuttings: cut healthy stem sections containing multiple nodes (about 5-10 cm) from the upper part of a mature stem, strip leaves from the lower nodes, and replant in nutrient-rich substrate. Trimming the top of an established stem encourages bushier lateral side-shoot growth on the mother plant. Seed propagation is not used in the hobby.

primary.name: Modern Aquarium
primary.notes: Used for scientific name (Nesaea pedicellata), West African origin, adult height (12 in / ~30 cm or more), moderate lighting baseline, CO2 not required but beneficial, moderate growth rate, nutrient-rich substrate with root tabs, midground-to-background placement, easy-to-moderate care level. Primary role assigned to Modern Aquarium because the Buce Plant URL supplied in the manifest (/products/nesaea-golden) returned HTTP 404; the live Buce listing at /products/nesaea-pedicellata-golden was instead used as a cross-reference via search snippets.

additional.name: HYGRONATURE — Ammannia pedicellata / Golden Nesaea Care Guide
additional.notes: Cross-checked Ammannia pedicellata synonymy, pH 6.0-7.5, hardness 2-10 dKH, high light (2 W/gal minimum, 8-10 h photoperiod), CO2 optional but beneficial at 20-30 ppm, fast growth (up to ~5 cm/week), stem-cutting propagation, nutrient-rich substrate with root tabs, iron-rich micronutrient dosing for golden coloration.

additional.name: Enchanted Grow — Nesaea Pedicellata (Golden)
additional.notes: Cross-checked temperature 22-28 C, pH 5.5-7.0 (lower than other sources), GH 2-12 dGH, high-intensity lighting 8-10 h/day, CO2 20-30 mg/L recommended, background/midground placement, moderate-to-fast growth, stem-cutting propagation with multiple nodes.

additional.name: GBIF — Ammannia pedicellata taxonomy
additional.notes: Cross-checked taxonomy: family Lythraceae, order Myrtales; confirms Nesaea pedicellata Hiern is a synonym of Ammannia pedicellata (Hiern) S.A.Graham & Gandhi (2013 revision folding Nesaea into Ammannia).
